# Source Card: The diversity of mutations and clinical outcomes for ELANE-associated neutropenia

## Summary
This SCNIR registry review (n=307 ELANE-mutated patients, 104 distinct mutations, up to 27 years follow-up) establishes the diagnostic ANC thresholds distinguishing cyclic neutropenia (CyN) from severe congenital neutropenia (SCN) and the genotype-phenotype associations that drive leukemic-transformation risk. For a pediatric CDS module it supplies (a) source-supported ANC diagnostic cutoffs for two congenital neutropenia phenotypes and (b) mutation-specific risk facts (frame-shift class, G214R, C151Y) usable as decision-support inputs. It is a genotype-phenotype outcomes paper, not a reference-interval study, so its numeric content is diagnostic cutoffs and event rates rather than age-partitioned reference intervals.

## Key evidence (with exact-passage locators)
- (ev_001) CyN defined by ANC <0.2 x10^9/L with ~21-day recurrence — locator: INTRODUCTION para 1 — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:546c959651480f51efa08ecd2f4672488dbeb51abddbf5eb19ef7283f2f3d9fe]"
- (ev_002) SCN usually ANC <0.5 x10^9/L + marrow maturation arrest — locator: INTRODUCTION para 1 — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:9be85f40fefda6725d04b0ba8c63231c23a3bf2e93d5552bd12700df87da4d3e]"
- (ev_003) CyN serial-count criterion near 2.0 down to <0.2 x10^9/L at ~21-day intervals — locator: METHODS 'SCNIR patients' — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:f3d189ae4a5867cf0a106a31a207ced36bb46d3389c31e56cfb857faab5c1c6f]"
- (ev_004) 30/187 SCN (16%) developed MDS/AML vs 0 CyN (p<0.0001); all frame-shift mutations in SCN — locator: OBSERVATIONS MDS/AML para — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:dbc12d0e6fc02f36cd7ee3fb921811c38aa96150a4348c45856171131f7c10b2]"
- (ev_005) 20-yr severe-event rate 100% for G214R and C151Y vs 0-10% for P139L/IVS4+5 G>A/S126L — locator: OBSERVATIONS G-CSF outcomes — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:e5cc8c5fa4abc32350e794aa82f5613a0ba3c4dda70f46d7c9e9f801af27ff10]"
- (ev_006) 20-yr cumulative severe-event incidence 46% SCN vs 7% CyN — locator: OBSERVATIONS G-CSF outcomes — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:79027bd928b7e0b24d68c4a9b21b0dabafded16c042203e3a56c2737f6300e96]"
- (ev_007) Cohort: 307 patients, 104 distinct mutations, up to 27 yr follow-up; sequencing predictive — locator: ABSTRACT Recent findings — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:3f9618f765eee2fb471b323817af7d5d7db48835a382c2b742980d7f5420041b]"

## Population / assay / lifecycle notes
- Population: pediatric-onset congenital neutropenias (CyN and SCN), both sexes, autosomal-dominant ELANE; no gestational-age/postnatal-age partitioning. ANC values are automated CBC-differential neutrophil counts; the source does NOT tie thresholds to a specific analyzer or method.
- Lifecycle: 2015 review (Current Opinion in Hematology); no guideline version/effective/retire dates asserted. ANC diagnostic cutoffs are stated as characteristic clinical criteria, not as a versioned guideline threshold.
- Units caveat: ANC passages render the exponent as "× 10/l"; the coefficient (0.2, 0.5, 2.0) is intact and the intended unit is x10^9/L (UCUM 10*9/L). Recorded verbatim; exponent loss noted as a limitation, not silently corrected.

## Scope-exit / referral relevance (if any)
- Provides risk-stratification inputs relevant to specialist referral: SCN vs CyN classification and high-risk genotypes (G214R, C151Y, frame-shift class) associated with markedly elevated MDS/AML and severe-event risk. These are prognostic facts for CDS, NOT a diagnostic/treatment directive.

## Limitations & conflicts
- Exponent rendering: the ANC threshold passages lost the superscript 9 ("× 10/l" instead of "×10^9/l") in PMC text extraction; numeric coefficients are verbatim and reliable, but the units digit is not itself quotable from the extracted text.
- No age-partitioned reference intervals: this source defines disease diagnostic cutoffs (CyN <0.2, SCN <0.5 x10^9/L), not age/sex reference ranges — a CALIPER-style partitioned CBC interval is NOT locatable here and must come from a reference-interval source.
- Registry-selection and diagnosis-assignment: authors note other groups found no genotype-outcome association ("Other researchers have concluded that there are no associations of genotypes and clinical outcomes"); this genotype-phenotype conflict is preserved, not resolved. No numeric conflict with other cards recorded.
- No transfusion/treatment/dosing directive is asserted; G-CSF dose figures in the source are descriptive registry data, deliberately NOT extracted as executable rules.
